microsoft_ft: Scaled Azure's recovery validation program from a 2-person operation into a **platformized system**, standardizing workflows to sustain 45+ annual resilience drills and drive **$14M+ business impact** across enterprise customers including Databricks, Walmart, SAP, and NetApp.

Led 0→1 development of an internal **Resilience Automation Platform**, defining self-service scheduling requirements that reduced operational toil by 30% and transitioned execution to a scalable, repeatable model across cross-org stakeholders.

Directed 14 cross-org recovery executions under executive visibility, serving as bridge lead for a sovereign-cloud **network isolation test** tied to a $1.5B+ enterprise contract, coordinating technical and field engineering teams across organizational boundaries.

Built an internal **AI agent** to automate drill planning workflows, reducing planning cycle time by 39% and increasing drill capacity by 21% — directly expanding the program's ability to support more customer engagements.

Pioneered Azure's first proactive rack-level resilience drill program, delivering in 4 months with a **94% recovery rate** and surfacing critical hardware defects that established a new continuous-validation model for industrial-scale infrastructure.
microsoft_2023: Championed adoption of **AI-driven automation workflows** by conducting live product demos and training sessions across 14 key Azure engineering teams, saving 37 engineering hours monthly and accelerating utilization of new tooling.

Influenced the product roadmap by facilitating **11+ user interviews** with Azure service teams to surface critical feature gaps, directly securing prioritization of intent-based YAML generation capabilities in the next release cycle.

Improved data retrieval and **system integration quality** by implementing rigorous metadata standards and migrating documentation to an AI-powered semantic search platform, cutting lookup time by 83% across engineering stakeholders.
microsoft_2022: Generated **$3M in accelerated revenue** and launched Azure regions 28% faster by driving cross-functional alignment on a unified automation prioritization framework across 140+ service teams, mirroring the cross-stakeholder coordination required in complex industrial deployments.

Conducted discovery with 20+ service teams to **quantify 81 hours of manual toil** per region launch, then built a roadmap to automate critical operational paths — translating field findings into a scalable, ROI-driven product strategy.
Engineered a **Power BI dashboard** tracking operational toil across 140+ teams, enabling leadership to identify automation gaps and prioritize high-impact improvements with data-driven precision.
amazon_robotics: Achieved **zero operational downtime** during a 2,000+ unit pilot transition by defining the legacy OS migration strategy and mapping dependencies across 1,200+ robotic stations — directly analogous to integrating legacy industrial control systems into modern networked environments.

Drove alignment between IT, Operations, and Engineering to implement **automated CI/CD pipelines**, accelerating the software deployment cycle by 25% and reducing manual intervention across distributed manufacturing floor assets.

Facilitated Agile sprint planning and retrospectives, resolving **40+ high-priority tickets** to stabilize the developer intake process and maintain continuous delivery cadence across a cross-functional robotics engineering team.
pro_painters: Increased job bookings by **26%** by managing the end-to-end scoping and invoicing lifecycle for 200+ monthly proposals through an optimized CRM process — demonstrating the HubSpot-based pipeline management central to this SE role.

Reduced **Customer Acquisition Cost by 13%** and boosted conversions by 2.7% by executing a digital-first go-to-market strategy, directly supporting Account Executives with technical and operational value framing for prospective clients.

Improved leadership pricing models by conducting **financial profitability analysis** across 1,000+ project records to identify and seal margin leaks, building the ROI quantification skills essential for articulating OEE business value to plant and C-level stakeholders.

tailoring_notes: Wove 'OEE / ROI quantification' into pro_painters bullet 3 and microsoft_2022 bullet 2, mirroring JD emphasis on articulating tangible ROI of complex technical solutions to both technical and non-technical audiences.

Used 'legacy industrial control systems / networked assets' framing in amazon_robotics bullet 1 to directly mirror JD language about 'legacy machines requiring creative data extraction to modern, networked assets' and PLC/SCADA integration experience.

Embedded 'HubSpot-based pipeline management' explicitly in pro_painters bullet 1 to surface the JD's preferred CRM tool, and 'Account Executives' in bullet 2 to mirror the JD's AE-partnering motion central to the SE role.

Used 'field engineering teams', 'customer engagements', and 'cross-org stakeholders' in microsoft_ft bullets to mirror JD language around travel support, field deployments, and multi-stakeholder technical leadership from plant engineers to C-suite.
